content:
<USER_REQUEST>
You need to create 3 CBSE Class 12 PHYSICS prediction papers for the 2027 board exam.

STEPS:
1. First, READ the latest SQP to understand the exact format:
   - Read file: D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\SQP 2025-26\Physics-SQP.pdf
   - Read file: D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\SQP 2025-26\Physics-MS.pdf
   - Also read 1-2 recent actual papers:
   - Read file: D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\2026\2383-1_55-1-1_Physics.pdf
   - Read file: D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\2025\55-1-1_Physics.pdf

2. ANALYZE patterns across the papers:
   - Section structure (Section A: MCQs, Section B: SA-I, Section C: SA-II, Section D: Case-based, Section E: LA)
   - Marks distribution per section
   - Which chapters/topics appear most frequently
   - Internal choice patterns
   - Types of numericals vs conceptual questions

3. CREATE 3 different prediction papers. Save them as:
   - D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\Prediction Papers\Physics_Prediction_Paper_1.md
   - D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\Prediction Papers\Physics_Prediction_Paper_2.md
   - D:\Study Papers\Jeffrey\Physics\Prediction Papers\Physics_Prediction_Paper_3.md

Each paper must:
- Follow the EXACT CBSE format from the SQP (same sections, same marks, same number of questions)
- Have COMPLETE, fully written questions (not just topic headings)
- Include diagrams described in text where needed (e.g., 'Draw a circuit diagram showing...')
- Cover different chapters/topics across the 3 papers
- Include proper internal choice (OR) questions
- Have appropriate difficulty mixing easy/medium/hard
- Include the standard CBSE header with time, marks, general instructions

Be thorough - these should be realistic enough to use for actual exam practice.
</USER_REQUEST>
<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>
The current local time is: 2026-06-14T18:40:28+05:30.
</ADDITIONAL_METADATA>

The search for "CBSE Class 12 Physics SQP 2025-26 sample question paper full questions section wise" returned the following summary:
The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has released the official **Sample Question Paper (SQP) and Marking Scheme for Class 12 Physics (2025-26)**[1][2].

### **Paper Structure & Overview**
The sample paper is designed to help students understand the exam pattern, question typology, and marking criteria for the 2026 board examinations[3][1].

*   **Total Marks:** 70[4][1]
*   **Time Allowed:** 3 Hours[4][1]
*   **Total Questions:** 33 (All questions are compulsory)[4][2]

### **Section-Wise Breakdown**
The paper is divided into five sections (A, B, C, D, and E)[4][5]:

| Section | Question Type | Number of Questions | Marks per Question |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Section A** | MCQs and Assertion-Reasoning | 16 | 1 Mark |
| **Section B** | Very Short Answer | 5 | 2 Marks |
| **Section C** | Short Answer | 7 | 3 Marks |
| **Section D** | Case-Study Based | 2 | 4 Marks |
| **Section E** | Long Answer | 3 | 5 Marks |

*(Note: Internal choices are provided in specific sections as per the board's standard pattern[6].)*

### **How to Access the Official Documents**
You can download the official PDF files directly from the CBSE Academic website[1]:

1.  Visit the official CBSE Academics website: **[cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)**[1]
2.  Navigate to the **"Sample Question Paper"** tab[7].
3.  Select **"Class XII"** and then the year **"2025-26"**[8][7].
4.  Find **"Physics (Code No. 042)"** to download both the **SQP** and the **Marking Scheme**[8][5][9].

Practicing these official papers is highly recommended to familiarize yourself with the increased weightage assigned to competency-based questions this year[2][10].

*   **Educational Websites:** Platforms such as SelfStudys, Vedantu, and others provide PDFs of the 2026 Physics question papers (including Set 55/1/1) along with solutions and exam analysis[4][5][2].

*   **Section B:** 5 questions, 2 marks each[9].

*   **Section D:** 2 case study-based questions, 4 marks each[9].

Created At: 2026-06-14T13:11:23Z

The search for "CBSE Class 12 Physics SQP 2025-26 all questions section A B C D E complete paper text" returned the following summary:
The official CBSE Class 12 Physics Sample Question Paper (SQP) for the 2025-26 academic session has been released by the Central Board of Secondary Education[1][2].

### **Question Paper Structure (2025-26)**
The theory paper carries a maximum of **70 marks** with a duration of **3 hours**[3][2]. The paper is divided into five sections (A, B, C, D, and E) with a total of **33 questions**[3][4].

*   **Section A:** 16 questions (12 MCQs and 4 Assertion-Reasoning based) of 1 mark each[5][4].
*   **Section B:** 5 questions of 2 marks each[5][4].
*   **Section C:** 7 questions of 3 marks each[5][4].
*   **Section D:** 2 case study-based questions of 4 marks each[5][4].
*   **Section E:** 3 long-answer questions of 5 marks each[4].

**Key Notes:**
*   **Internal Choices:** There is no overall choice in the paper; however, internal choices are provided in two questions in Section B, one question in Section C, and all three questions in Section E[4].
*   **Official Access:** You can download the complete official PDF of the Sample Question Paper and the Marking Scheme directly from the **[CBSE Academic Website](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)**[2]. 

It is recommended to use the official CBSE portal to ensure you are practicing with the authentic document, as it contains the precise marking scheme and the latest competency-based question typology mandated for the 2026 board examinations[6][7].

The search for "CBSE Class 12 Physics 2025-26 marking scheme chapter wise weightage unit wise marks distribution" returned the following summary:
For the CBSE Class 12 Physics examination (2025-26), the theory paper carries **70 marks**, with an additional **30 marks** allocated for practical and internal assessment, totaling 100 marks.

### **Unit-Wise Marks Distribution (2025-26)**

The syllabus is organized into nine units across 14 chapters. The distribution of marks for the theory paper is as follows:

| Unit | Unit Name | Marks |
| :--- | :--- | :---: |
| I | Electrostatics | 16 |
| II | Current Electricity | 16 |
| III | Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism | 17 |
| IV | Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents | 17 |
| V | Electromagnetic Waves | |
| VI | Optics | 14* |
| VII | Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter | |
| VIII | Atoms and Nuclei | 8 |
| IX | Electronic Devices | 7 |
| **Total** | | **70** |

*\*Note: While specific distributions for smaller units may vary slightly in combined allocations, Units I-IV typically form the high-weightage core of the syllabus (collectively accounting for a significant portion of the marks).*

---

### **Exam Pattern Highlights**
*   **Total Duration:** 3 Hours.
*   **Question Paper Design:**
    *   **20% MCQs:** Objective-type questions.
    *   **30% Competency-based Questions:** Including case-based, source-based, or integrated questions.
    *   **50% Short Answer/Long Answer Questions:** Standard theory and numerical problems.
*   **Practical Assessment (30 Marks):** Includes experiments, record/practical file, and viva voce.

### **Preparation Tips**
*   **Focus on High-Weightage Areas:** Units like Electrostatics, Current Electricity, Magnetic Effects, and Optics are traditionally the most significant in terms of marks.
*   **Conceptual Clarity:** CBSE emphasizes understanding over rote learning. Prioritize derivations, diagrams, and the application of physical principles.
*   **Official Resources:** For the most accurate and updated information, including detailed chapter-wise topics and practical guidelines, you can visit the official [CBSE Academic website](https://cbseacademic.nic.in/).

The search for "CBSE Class 12 Physics SQP 2025-26 questions with answers Section A MCQ electrostatics optics" returned the following summary:
The official **CBSE Class 12 Physics Sample Question Paper (SQP) for the 2025–26 academic session** has been released[1][2] and is available on the official CBSE academic website (`cbseacademic.nic.in`)[3][4].

### **Exam Pattern Overview (Section A)**
According to the official SQP, **Section A** is structured as follows[5][6]:
*   **Total Questions:** 16 questions[7][6].
*   **Type:** 12 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and 4 Assertion-Reasoning based questions[5][6].
*   **Weightage:** 1 mark each[5][7].

### **Sample Topics & Questions (Electrostatics & Optics)**
Based on the provided sample materials, here are examples of the types of questions found in the 2025-26 curriculum for these units:

#### **Electrostatics (Representative Examples)**
*   **Concept:** Electrostatic Equilibrium
    *   *Question:* If a charged hollow sphere and a solid sphere of aluminum and copper of equal radii are in electrostatic equilibrium, which statement is true?[5]
    *   *Answer:* Both spheres have equal charges (because for two bodies to be in equilibrium, they must be at the same potential)[8].
*   **Concept:** Electric Potential & Capacitance
    *   *Question:* Electric potential $V$ due to an electric dipole is related to the distance $r$ as:[9]
    *   *Answer:* $V \propto r^{-2}$[9].
    *   *Question:* What happens to the energy stored in a parallel plate capacitor when a dielectric slab is inserted after disconnecting the battery?[9]

#### **Optics**
While specific optics MCQs vary by set, the syllabus covers:
*   **Ray Optics and Optical Instruments:** Focuses on reflection, refraction, lenses, and optical instruments[3].
*   **Wave Optics:** Includes interference, diffraction, and polarization[3].
*   *Note:* Ensure you practice questions involving Snell’s Law, lens maker’s formula, and Young’s Doubl

The search for "CBSE Class 12 Physics unit wise marks distribution 2025-26 blueprint detailed chapters" returned the following summary:
For the CBSE Class 12 Physics examination (2025-26), the theory paper consists of **70 marks**[1][2][3], with an additional **30 marks** allocated for practical assessment[1][2][3]. The syllabus is organized into 9 units covering 14 chapters[2][4][3].

### **CBSE Class 12 Physics Unit-Wise Marks Distribution (2025-26)**

| Unit | Unit Name | Chapters Covered | Marks |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :---: |
| **I** | Electrostatics | Ch-1: Electric Charges and Fields; Ch-2: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance | 16 |
| **II** | Current Electricity | Ch-3: Current Electricity | 16 |
| **III** | Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism | Ch-4: Moving Charges and Magnetism; Ch-5: Magnetism and Matter | 17 |
| **IV** | Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents | Ch-6: Electromagnetic Induction; Ch-7: Alternating Current | 17 |
| **V** | Electromagnetic Waves | Ch-8: Electromagnetic Waves | (Combined) |
| **VI** | Optics | Ch-9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments; Ch-10: Wave Optics | (Combined) |
| **VII** | Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter | Ch-11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter | (Combined) |
| **VIII** | Atoms and Nuclei | Ch-12: Atoms; Ch-13: Nuclei | (Combined) |
| **IX** | Electronic Devices | Ch-14: Semiconductor Electronics | (Combined) |

***Note:** While some units are grouped for marks, the total theory paper is **70 marks**[2][4][3]. Units V, VI, VII, VIII, and IX collectively carry the remaining marks to complete the 70-mark total. Detailed weightage may vary slightly by board-released blueprints; please refer to the latest official CBSE academic curriculum for specific sub-unit breakdowns if provided.*

### **Exam Pattern Highlights**
*   **Total Marks:** 100 (70 Theory + 30 Practical)[2][3].
*   **Exam Duration:** 3 Hours[2][4].
*   **Question Paper Structure:**
    *   **20%** Competency-based questions[2][4].
    *   **40%** Short and Long Answer questions[2][4].
    *   **40%** MCQs and Assertion-Reason questions[2][4].
*   **Passing Criteria:** Students must secure a minimum of **33% marks** in theory and practical components separately to pass the subject[5][6].

The official CBSE Class 12 Physics Sample Question Paper (SQP) for the 2025-26 academic session has been released by the Central Board of Secondary Education[1][2].

### Accessing the Official Question Paper
To ensure you have the authentic document with the correct marking scheme, it is recommended to download it directly from the official CBSE academic website:
*   **Official CBSE Academic Website:** [cbseacademic.nic.in](https://cbseacademic.nic.in)[1]
*   **Navigation:** Go to the "Sample Question Paper" section, select "Class XII," and then choose "2025-26" to find the Physics SQP and its corresponding Marking Scheme (MS)[3][4].

### Paper Structure Summary
As per the official 2025-26 guidelines, the Physics question paper consists of **33 questions**[5][2] divided into five sections[5][6]:

*   **Section A:** 16 questions (12 MCQs and 4 Assertion-Reasoning based), 1 mark each[5][2].
*   **Section B:** 5 very short answer (VSA) questions, 2 marks each[5][6].
*   **Section C:** 7 short answer (SA) questions, 3 marks each[5][7].
*   **Section D:** 2 case study-based questions, 4 marks each[5][7].
*   **Section E:** 3 long answer questions, 5 marks each[5][7].

*Note: While many third-party platforms like Scribd, Vedantu, or coaching websites host versions of these papers[8][9][10], always verify the content against the official CBSE Academic portal to ensure you are practicing with the most accurate and updated version of the curriculum[11][2].*

The general instructions for the CBSE Class 12 Physics (Code No. 042) Sample Question Paper for the 2025-26 academic session are as follows:

### **General Instructions**
1.  There are 33 questions in all. All questions are compulsory.
2.  This question paper has five sections: Section A, Section B, Section C, Section D and Section E.
3.  All the sections are compulsory.
4.  **Section A** contains sixteen questions, twelve MCQ and four assertion reasoning based of 1 mark each.
5.  **Section B** contains five questions of two marks each.
6.  **Section C** contains seven questions of three marks each.
7.  **Section D** contains two case study-based questions of four marks each.
8.  **Section E** contains three long answer questions of five marks each.
9.  **There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two questions in Section B, one question in Section C and all three questions in Section E.** You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
10. Use of calculators is not allowed.

***

*Note: The exact wording regarding internal choices in the official CBSE SQP 2025-26 is: "There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in two question in Section B, one question in Section C and all three questions in Section E. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions."*
